questions.text: Who makes laws for the country?
questions.explanation: The government makes laws and takes decisions for the welfare of the people. In India, Parliament makes laws at the national level.

questions.text: India is a democracy where people elect their representatives.
questions.explanation: India is a democratic republic where citizens above 18 years elect their representatives through free and fair elections.

questions.text: Name any two things that the government does for people.
questions.correctAnswer: Building roads and schools (or any two government functions)
questions.explanation: Government performs many functions including building infrastructure (roads, bridges), providing education (schools), healthcare (hospitals), and maintaining law and order.

questions.text: The system where people elect their rulers is called ________.
questions.explanation: Democracy is a form of government where citizens elect their representatives through voting. The word comes from Greek 'demos' (people) and 'kratos' (rule).
